title: Webhooks, Replay Defense, SSRF, Unsafe Consumption, and Business Abuse · Contract Lens: Unsafe Upstream Consumption
summary: A gateway parses before signature verification, accepts stale duplicates, follows arbitrary callback URLs, trusts upstream JSON, and protects expensive workflows only with request counts. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts unsafe upstream consumption increment in signed ingress, bounded egress, and abuse decision model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Authenticate upstreams, bound redirects, time and bytes, validate response type and schema, sanitize content, minimize authority, and contain upstream failure.
Allowlist scheme and port, canonicalize host, resolve and revalidate every destination, block private or metadata ranges, control redirects, and enforce egress network policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Predict Unsafe Upstream Consumption · Theory Unsafe Upstream Consumption
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 4789 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to combine signed ingress freshness, replay defense, and SSRF egress policy; keep rollback safe during a storage expansion; then saturate one tenant budget while another tenant remains serviceable. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Unsafe Upstream Consumption contract for the supplied data.
